deep_dive.title: GEMS: Geometric Constraints Enable Multi-Semantic Superposition in LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Activation steering controls model behavior by modifying intermediate hidden states at inference time without retraining. Existing methods handle only single-direction injection; when multiple semantic directions are superposed without constraints, the model collapses. We show that this collapse decomposes into two independently acting sources: distributional deviation, where additive perturbations accumulate in norm across layers and drive activations outside the training distribution, and directional interference, where non-orthogonal semantic vectors mutually dampen when superposed. These two sources define the design constraints that any training-free multi-directional intervention must address. As one instantiation of these principles, we propose GEMS, a training-free method that maps each source to a corresponding geometric constraint: norm-preserving weighted superposition and targeted attention-pathway injection for distributional deviation, and real-time orthogonalization for directional interference. On GSM8K, injecting three concurrent non-mathematical directions preserves accuracy at 98% (baseline 92%), while unconstrained addition collapses to 4%; on Wikitext-2, the same injection incurs only 2.2% PPL increase. Component ablation isolates the causal role of each constraint, and layer-level probes confirm that orthogonalized signals survive the FFN pathway and reach the output distribution with semantic specificity. Qualitative steering effects transfer across architectures from 3B to 31B.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 专注于激活引导的大语言模型多语义控制，直接相关于激活引导需求
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 专注于激活引导的大语言模型多语义控制，直接相关于激活引导需求
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 专注于激活引导的大语言模型多语义控制，直接相关于激活引导需求
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: GEMS proposes a constraint-based framework for multi-semantic superposition in activation steering, enabling training-free multi-directional control of LLMs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文指出，现有激活引导方法仅支持单向注入，多方向叠加会导致模型崩溃。GEMS将这种崩溃分解为分布偏差和方向干扰两类来源，并基于几何约束提出一种无训练的框架，实现大语言模型的多语义叠加控制。实验表明该方法能有效协调多个语义方向，为安全数据生成等应用提供支持。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文指出，现有激活引导方法仅支持单向注入，多方向叠加会导致模型崩溃。GEMS将这种崩溃分解为分布偏差和方向干扰两类来源，并基于几何约束提出一种无训练的框架，实现大语言模型的多语义叠加控制。实验表明该方法能有效协调多个语义方向，为安全数据生成等应用提供支持。

quick_skim.title: SPARK: Security Knowledge Priming and Representation-Guided Knowledge Activation for LLM-based Secure Code Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models routinely generate code with exploitable security flaws. Prior literature attributes this limitation to a lack of security expertise, steering current defense mechanisms toward heavy fine-tuning or external knowledge retrieval, which introduces significant computational overhead and data bias through redundant code examples. Contrary to this view, we argue that pretraining corpora are already rich in security material. The bottleneck is activation: without an explicit and brief cue, statistical pressure toward common training-distribution patterns suppresses the model's safety-relevant representations. We present SPARK, an inference-time security harness that activates this latent knowledge without any retraining. The harness has two parts. Component~I retrieves a few of the relevant Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) entries for each coding task and appends a short structured cue to the prompt; this alone is enough to surface the model's existing security representations. Component~II adds a precomputed token bias to the logits at every decoding step. We obtain the bias by projecting a safe-direction vector, the unit difference between the mean safe and mean unsafe last-layer hidden states, through the language model head. The bias is computed once offline; applying it costs a single vector addition per generated token. We evaluate SPARK on 9 open-source models across C++, Java, and Python, and compare with 7 baselines spanning fine-tuning and retrieval-augmented methods. SPARK matches or improves on the best baseline in every setting while preserving HumanEval utility. We further test Component~I in a black-box setting on 7 of today's strongest models, including Claude, DeepSeek, and GPT, demonstrating the bottleneck of insecure code generation and the improvements enabled by our method.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses representation-guided knowledge activation for security, similar to activation steering for safety data generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用表示引导的知识激活来增强安全性，类似于用于安全数据生成的激活引导
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用表示引导的知识激活来增强安全性，类似于用于安全数据生成的激活引导
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用表示引导的知识激活来增强安全性，类似于用于安全数据生成的激活引导
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SPARK, an inference-time security harness that activates latent security knowledge in LLMs via explicit brief cues, avoiding heavy fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对LLM代码生成中的安全漏洞问题，提出SPARK推理时安全工具。不同于现有依赖微调或外部知识的方法，SPARK通过简洁的显式提示激活模型预训练语料中已有的安全知识，避免计算开销和数据偏差。实验表明，SPARK能有效提升安全代码生成性能，且无需额外训练。该方法展示了激活引导在安全增强中的潜力，可迁移至安全数据生成场景。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对LLM代码生成中的安全漏洞问题，提出SPARK推理时安全工具。不同于现有依赖微调或外部知识的方法，SPARK通过简洁的显式提示激活模型预训练语料中已有的安全知识，避免计算开销和数据偏差。实验表明，SPARK能有效提升安全代码生成性能，且无需额外训练。该方法展示了激活引导在安全增强中的潜力，可迁移至安全数据生成场景。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Activation steering for synthetic safety data generation
